summary: Feature Testing Complete: Products Sorting, Price History Enhanced, and Pagination. Backend: 19/19 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All features verified working correctly.

issues: Minor: recharts console warnings about width/height -1, charts still render correctly

passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/products-unified without sorting returns products
Backend: GET /api/products-unified?sort_by=name&sort_order=asc sorts by name ascending
Backend: GET /api/products-unified?sort_by=name&sort_order=desc sorts by name descending
Backend: GET /api/products-unified?sort_by=price&sort_order=asc sorts by price ascending
Backend: GET /api/products-unified?sort_by=price&sort_order=desc sorts by price descending
Backend: GET /api/products-unified?sort_by=stock&sort_order=asc sorts by stock ascending
Backend: GET /api/products-unified?sort_by=suppliers&sort_order=desc sorts by supplier count descending
Backend: GET /api/products-unified/count returns total count for pagination
Backend: GET /api/products-unified pagination (skip/limit) works correctly
Backend: GET /api/products-unified pagination with sorting combined works
Backend: GET /api/price-history/top-products returns products with most price changes
Backend: GET /api/price-history/top-products respects limit parameter
Backend: GET /api/price-history/top-products sorted by changes descending
Backend: GET /api/price-history/product/{product_name} returns evolution structure
Backend: GET /api/price-history/product/{product_name} timeline format correct for recharts
Backend: GET /api/price-history/product/NonExistent returns empty timeline
Backend: GET /api/price-history returns list of price changes with correct structure
Backend: GET /api/price-history without auth returns 401
Frontend: Products page loads with sorting buttons in table headers
Frontend: Sort by name (Producto) button works - toggles ASC/DESC with arrow indicators
Frontend: Sort by price (Mejor Precio) button works with visual indicator
Frontend: Sort by stock (Stock Total) button works
Frontend: Sort by suppliers (Proveedores) button works
Frontend: Pagination controls visible (page numbers, next/prev buttons)
Frontend: Pagination next button navigates to next page
Frontend: Page size selector present (data-testid='page-size-select')
Frontend: Price History page loads with title 'Historial de Precios'
Frontend: Area chart 'Cambios de precio (últimos 14 días)' displays with red/green areas
Frontend: 'Productos más activos' section shows top products with change counts and percentages
Frontend: Clicking top product opens 'Evolución de precio' dialog with line chart
Frontend: Product evolution dialog shows current/min/max prices and timeline chart
Frontend: Price history table shows PRODUCTO, PRECIO ANTERIOR, PRECIO NUEVO, VARIACIÓN, FECHA columns
Frontend: Search input for price history present (data-testid='search-price-history')
Frontend: Days filter dropdown present (data-testid='filter-days')
critical_code_review_comments: Products.jsx correctly implements sorting with sort_by and sort_order params passed to backend
Backend products.py products-unified endpoint correctly handles sorting via MongoDB aggregation pipeline
recharts LineChart used for individual product evolution, AreaChart for overview
normalize_product_data in sync.py has comprehensive alias mapping for common CSV column names
FtpFileBrowser.jsx uses corrected endpoint /api/suppliers/ftp-browse for FTP directory browsing

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all sorting, pagination, and price history features verified)

seed_data_creation: 83 test products and 31 price history entries already seeded by main agent
context_for_next_testing_agent: Products sorting data-testid: sort-name, sort-price, sort-stock, sort-suppliers. Pagination: pagination-next, pagination-prev, pagination-first, pagination-last, page-size-select. Price History: price-history-title, search-price-history, filter-days, top-product-{idx}. Current suppliers use URL Directa connection (not FTP), so FTP browser testing requires creating FTP-configured supplier. The normalize_product_data function requires either standard column names (sku/codigo, name/nombre) or explicit column_mapping configuration for non-standard CSV files.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A - All features working correctly. Note: The user's concern about 'FTP with 1 CSV not recognizing products' relates to normalize_product_data requiring either (1) standard column names in CSV, or (2) manual column_mapping configuration. The function logs warnings when SKU/Name cannot be detected.
